title: The Ontology of Coexistence
description: Existence, what exists, beginninglessness, self persistence, world reality, saturation, causation, orders, sentience, and conservation — Madhyasth Darshan compared with Advaita Vedanta and modern philosophy

title: Nature of Time
description: The nature of time (kaal), timeless satta, activity and duration, and comparison with Advaita, philosophy of time, and physics

title: Why Humans Are Not Just Material
description: Madhyasth Darshan, science, and Advaita Vedanta on whether humans are more than matter

title: Philosophy of Mind and Jeevan
description: Jeevan, mun, vritti, chitta, buddhi, atma, desire, imagination, selection, delusion, and awakening

title: Knowledge Knower and Known
description: Comparative study of knowledge, knower, and known in Madhyasth Darshan, Advaita Vedanta, and Modern Philosophy

title: Methodology and Hermeneutics
description: Primary-text method, translation issues, citation discipline, interpretation, comparison, and rigorous study practice

title: Axiology Value Theory
description: Values, value-recognition, value-fulfilment, mutual satisfaction, and the structure of humane value theory

title: Ethics and Morals in Human Beings
description: Ethics, morals, humane conduct, Advaita Vedanta, and modern moral science

title: Family Relationships and Values
description: Family, relationship-recognition, values, trust, affection, gratitude, and mutual satisfaction

title: Education and Sanskar
description: Education, sanskar, study, awakening, and how understanding is transmitted across generations

title: Aesthetics
description: Aesthetics, beauty, art, Advaita Vedanta, and modern philosophy from a Madhyasth Darshan standpoint

title: Human Behavior and Society
description: Behaviour, ethics, and social organisation in the darshan

title: How to Form Self-Sustaining Organizations
description: Organisations that sustain without instrumentalising their members

title: Governance Justice and Undivided Society
description: Justice, governance, law, fearlessness, public order, and undivided society

title: Prosperity Economics and Right Use
description: Prosperity, production, cyclical economics, right-use of wealth, and limits to accumulation

title: Nature Ecology and Right Use
description: Nature, Earth, right-use, regeneration, ecological balance, and coexistence with the other orders

title: Spiritual Practice and Realization
description: Anubhav, realization, awakening, yoga, liberation from mystery, liberation from suffering, and comparison with moksha traditions

title: Science Technology and Human Purpose
description: Science, technology, production, convenience, human purpose, and the limits of instrumental knowledge
